title: Proposal: online reliability rollback penalty gate
linkedMetricEvidence: Loop B policy online advantage report 2026-05-31T19:48:00Z: construction-priority.territory-shadow.v1 and expansion-remote.territory-shadow.v1 passed offline/shadow evaluation but were rolled back in official MMO because online reliability dropped about 17-18%, above the configured 10% rollback threshold.
Runtime console ticks 1623386-1623404 disabled the candidate families after reliability drops around 17.0-18.4% versus the 10.0% threshold.
linkedDashboardPanels: #924 candidate-vs-baseline scorecard: reliability delta and rollback threshold
problemStatement: Offline/shadow evaluation can currently admit candidate families that later fail online reliability. In the 2026-05-31 Loop B window, construction-priority.territory-shadow.v1 and expansion-remote.territory-shadow.v1 passed offline/shadow evaluation but were disabled after official MMO reliability dropped about 17-18%, exceeding the configured 10% rollback threshold. A durable reward-governance decision is needed so future #924 candidate-vs-baseline comparisons reject this failure mode before candidate admission proceeds.
hypothesis: Treating online or shadow reliability regression as a fail-closed candidate admission gate, rather than a compensable reward term, will prevent promotion of candidates whose reliability drop exceeds the configured rollback threshold while preserving the project priority order: reliability/safety first, then territory, resources, and kills.
currentRewardCoverage: Reliability is already documented as a safety floor in reward and rollout guidance, and #924 is the candidate-vs-baseline scorecard contract. No accepted reward component currently turns online rollback evidence into a durable admission rejection rule for candidate families that pass offline/shadow utility but fail online reliability. This record is proposal-only and does not authorize live reward changes, official MMO learned-policy writes, or paid Tencent validation.
direction: hard_gate; fail closed when online or shadow reliability drop exceeds the configured rollback threshold, currently 10.0% versus baseline
expectedBehaviorChange: The #924 candidate-vs-baseline scorecard rejects or marks ROLLBACK_REQUIRED for a candidate family when online or shadow reliability drop exceeds the configured rollback threshold, even if offline utility, territory, resource, or kill metrics improve. The first implementation recommendation is candidate admission/scorecard gating only. Reward shaping and scenario weighting remain future shadow-only follow-ups unless later evidence supports them.
riskAndRegressions: A reliability gate with ambiguous baseline/candidate windows can reject useful candidates or pass unsafe candidates for the wrong reason.
Reward shaping based directly on online rollback could overfit to a small rollback window before enough shadow evidence exists.
Scenario weighting could hide reliability failures if it changes dataset mix before the #924 scorecard gate is enforced.
Paid Tencent validation while #1548, #1536, or Tencent recurrence gates are blocked would violate the current compute safety boundary.
Any live learned-policy write or official MMO control surface would exceed this offline/shadow governance record.

validationWindows.name: loop_b_rollback_evidence_replay
requiredEvidence: runtime-artifacts/rl-control-loop/20260531T194800Z-policy-advantage.json or newer policy advantage artifact referencing the same rollback window
scorecard outcome would fail closed for both candidate families

validationWindows.name: candidate_vs_baseline_scorecard_gate
requiredEvidence: baseline reliability and candidate reliability over the same comparison window
computed reliability drop or reliability delta
fail-closed result when reliability fields, baseline windows, candidate windows, or threshold are missing
CPU bucket, loop exception, telemetry freshness, room survival, and spawn survival non-regression

validationWindows.name: post_gate_experiment_card
requiredEvidence: created only after #1548, #1536, and Tencent recurrence gates are unblocked
no paid Tencent compute before those gates clear
offline/shadow-only experiment card comparing incumbent baseline against the gated candidate family
candidate admission gate enabled before any reward shaping or scenario weighting trial

acceptanceCriteria: Proposal remains offline/shadow governance only until a later owner/steward decision approves implementation.
#924 candidate-vs-baseline scorecards fail closed when online or shadow reliability drop exceeds the configured rollback threshold; default threshold is 10.0% until superseded by another decision.

The 2026-05-31 Loop B rollback evidence would be rejected by the gate because both construction-priority.territory-shadow.v1 and expansion-remote.territory-shadow.v1 dropped reliability about 17.0-18.4%, above the 10.0% threshold.
First implementation is candidate admission/scorecard gating. Reward shaping and scenario weighting are not accepted by this decision and remain future shadow-only follow-ups.

Validation covers reliability delta, #924 scorecard outcome, rollback threshold, CPU bucket, loop exceptions, telemetry freshness, room survival, spawn survival, and no official MMO learned-policy writes.
No paid Tencent validation runs while #1548, #1536, or Tencent recurrence gates are blocked.
All validation artifacts preserve liveEffect:false, officialMmoWrites:false, officialMmoWritesAllowed:false, and all live-control safety flags false.
rollbackCriteria: Owner-independent rejection: candidate reliability drop exceeds the configured rollback threshold in online or shadow evidence.
Owner-independent rejection: #924 scorecard lacks baseline reliability, candidate reliability, comparison window, configured rollback threshold, or fail-closed status.
Owner-independent rejection: candidate improves territory, resources, or kills only by taking a reliability, CPU, room-survival, spawn-survival, loop-exception, or telemetry-freshness regression.
Owner-independent rejection: implementation attempts reward shaping or scenario weighting before candidate admission gating is proven.
Owner-independent rejection: validation requires paid Tencent compute while #1548, #1536, or Tencent recurrence gates remain blocked.
Owner-independent rejection: any official MMO learned-policy write, live control surface, Memory write, RawMemory write, creep intent, spawn intent, construction intent, or market intent is enabled.
Owner rejects, suspends, or supersedes the decision.

scorecardGate.validationMetric: candidate online/shadow reliability drop versus baseline over the same comparison window
scorecardGate.requiredOutcome: Reject candidate admission when reliability drop exceeds threshold, and reject when required reliability fields or threshold are missing.

implementationRecommendation.reason: The evidence is a rollback safety failure after offline/shadow pass, so the smallest safe slice is a #924 fail-closed admission gate. Reward shaping or scenario weighting could be explored later only after gated shadow evidence shows the admission rule is stable.

postGateExperimentPlan: After #1548, #1536, and Tencent recurrence gates are unblocked, create an offline/shadow experiment card for the first compute-resumed run.

Use incumbent baseline versus construction-priority.territory-shadow.v1 and expansion-remote.territory-shadow.v1, or their current successor family IDs, over the rollback-evidence window plus a fresh matched shadow window.

Record rollbackThresholdPct=10.0, baselineReliability, candidateReliability, reliabilityDeltaPct, scorecardStatus, CPU bucket, loop exceptions, telemetry freshness, room survival, spawn survival, territory, resources, and kills.
Enable candidate admission gating before any reward shaping or scenario weighting variant.
Treat any reliability drop above threshold, missing reliability field, missing baseline/candidate window, CPU/room safety regression, or live-write requirement as a fail-closed rejection.
Only after the admission gate rejects the known bad rollback window and passes a clean shadow comparison may a separate decision propose reward shaping or scenario weighting.

stewardDecision.notes: Proposal only. The conservative first implementation is #924 candidate admission/scorecard gating. No reward shaping, scenario weighting, paid Tencent validation, or live official MMO learned-policy writes are approved.

ownerDecision.notes: No accepted reward change and no live official MMO authority.

linkedPolicyEvaluations: pending: first post-gate offline/shadow experiment card after #1548/#1536/Tencent recurrence gates clear
